content:
This Rapido driver left me speechless.

It was an hour-long ride, and I noticed a few books tucked beside the driver's seat. Out of curiosity, I picked one up. It was study material for the Bihar Public Service Commission (BPSC). The driver looked like he was in his twenties, so I asked, "Ye books aapki hain?"

He smiled and said, "Haan, BPSC ki preparation kar raha hoon." Then he added, "Kabhi rides nahi milti to padh leta hoon. Khana khata hoon tab padh leta hoon. Ya to ride, ya phir padhai."

He told me he had moved to Delhi from Bihar with his father. His father works as a security guard, while he drives Rapido. His dream is to clear the BPSC exam and move back home with his father so the entire family can stay together and so he can do something meaningful for the people of his state.

That ride reminded me that our circumstances don't always decide our future. Our choices often do.

I really hope he clears the exam one day. ✨
comment: Stories like this also highlight the need for better support systems for working aspirants—flexible learning access, guidance, and pathways that make ambition sustainable alongside survival.
